executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 101 Canadian organizations compromised in the last 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 19 victims, followed by SafePay (13), Play (10), and Clop (13). Critical infrastructure including healthcare, education, financial services, and municipal government have been severely impacted.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 19 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Waterloo Information Systems, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Kuzco Lighting, Traffic Tech, Service Star Freightways, and multiple law/accounting firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1204 (User Execution)

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including Teccart educational institution, multiple construction firms (Groupe Piche, Kenalex, Silverline Group), manufacturing (Precision Aluminum), and transportation (Briway Carriers)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 10 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Select Tool, and Pewarchuk CPA
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1047 (Windows Management Instrumentation)

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including law firm SMT Lawyers, technology firms (Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Technikel), helicopter services (CMH Heli), manufacturers (Ideal Welders), and hospitality (Whiskijack Resorts)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Esposito Bros Construction, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, Emond Publishing, Phoenix Labs, CCR Solutions, and Soteck
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021 (Remote Services)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Labtician Ophthalmics (Healthcare), ControlGMC (Manufacturing), Groupe Caddac, and Real Tech
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, KTL Group, and Trucash
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1070 (Indicator Removal)

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Active Green + Ross, Morison Insurance Brokers, Kids & Company, and The Sundher Group

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a catastrophic ransomware crisis with 101 organizations compromised across all provinces in a 48-hour period. Critical sectors hit hardest include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Thunder Bay Counselling, BioPharma Services, multiple medical clinics), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart, Collge Superieur De Montreal), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, Morison Insurance Brokers). No CCCS advisories were issued during this period despite the unprecedented attack volume.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), indicating a major security breach. Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) and Kids & Company (childcare services) were also compromised. No incidents involving Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, or Shoppers Drug Mart identified.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a confirmed database leak by the Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers was compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting firms hit including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and Bergmanis Preyra law/accounting. No incidents involving major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) identified.

report_2w.executive_summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) has issued an advisory for a critical vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616), requiring immediate attention. Separately, Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 is being actively exploited in the wild and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Cisco ISE has a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-20147) allowing authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code. Akira ransomware has breached Nobu Restaurant Group, and a new AI-powered vishing platform called ATHR is enabling automated voice phishing attacks. No incidents affecting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.

items.description: Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability under active exploitation
items.cia_impact: Unknown - added to CISA KEV catalog

items.description: Cisco ISE and Cisco ISE-PIC remote code execution vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: FastGPT AI Agent platform password-based login endpoint vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: FastGPT AI Agent platform password change endpoint vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Nordic Semiconductor IronSide SE for nRF54H20 algorithmic complexity issue
items.software_affected: Nordic Semiconductor IronSide SE for nRF54H20 before 23.0.2+17

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Nobu Restaurant Group
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - Ransomware deployment with data exfiltration

items.name: Agent Tesla
items.targets: General targeting - 16 new samples identified
items.ttps: T1056 (Input Capture), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) - Information stealer with keylogging capabilities

items.name: ATHR Vishing Platform
items.targets: General public through automated AI voice phishing
items.ttps: T1566.004 (Phishing: Spear Phishing Voice) - AI-powered automated voice phishing attacks

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Advisory AL26-007 on April 7, 2026, regarding a vulnerability affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616). No breaches or incidents involving major Canadian retailers (Loblaws, Canadian Tire, Shoppers Drug Mart) or financial institutions (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) were reported during this period. A study by researchers in Alberta and British Columbia found climate change impacts on wildfire patterns, but this was not cybersecurity-related.
